title: Diagram Viewer v1 Rebuild (epic)
content:
Parent epic for the v1 rebuild of the diagram viewer. Implements [[s-9gls]]{ implements }.

This epic rolls up six sequential phases as child issues. See `TODO.md` at the project root for the full plan and `s-9gls` for the design.

## Phases

1. Project scaffolding (go.mod, Taskfile, Caddyfile, index.html, fixture)
2. Decompose monolith into parent + three sub-components (no behaviour change)
3. localStorage persistence (UI state + manifest)
4. JSON import/export dialog + Reset toolbar button
5. Public API: `loadData(data)` (v0 shape only)
6. Playwright-go test suite (one file per component, full coverage)

The leaf phase (6) blocks this epic so the rollup waits for all phases to finish before being marked ready.

## Definition of done

- All v0 user-facing behaviour preserved (no regression)
- `task build` produces `dist/diagram-viewer.{js,css}`
- `task serve` renders the Kubernetes example with full functionality
- `task test` passes the full Playwright-go suite
- localStorage persists UI state + manifest across reloads
- JSON import/export round-trips correctly
- Implementation feedback added to `s-9gls` summarising results

title: Phase 1: Project scaffolding
content:
Set up Go-tooled build system mirroring story-mapper. Implements Phase 1 of s-9gls.

## Tasks

- Create `go.mod` with `module github.com/metafeather-org/diagram-viewer`, Go 1.26+, and `tool` directives for `github.com/evanw/esbuild/cmd/esbuild` and `github.com/caddyserver/caddy/v2/cmd/caddy`
- Add `github.com/playwright-community/playwright-go` as a non-tool require for tests
- Run `go mod tidy` to populate `go.sum`
- Create `Taskfile.yaml` with `default` (list-all), `env`, `build`, `dev`, `test`, `serve`, `clean` — modelled on story-mapper, bundling `src/diagram-viewer.js` → `dist/diagram-viewer.js` and `src/styles.css` → `dist/diagram-viewer.css`
- Create `Caddyfile` serving project root on `:8080` with permissive CORS headers
- Create `index.html` at project root: `<diagram-viewer></diagram-viewer>`, links `dist/diagram-viewer.css`, imports `dist/diagram-viewer.js`, inline module script that fetches `examples/kubernetes/manifest.json` and calls `loadData(data)`
- Add `.gitignore` covering `dist/`, `node_modules/`, Playwright artefacts
- Copy v0 `viewer/example/` and `viewer/manifest.json` into `examples/kubernetes/` as a known-good fixture for both manual use and tests

## Definition of done

`task build` succeeds and produces empty stub bundles; `task serve` starts Caddy on `:8080`. The Kubernetes fixture is in place. No JS/CSS sources written yet — that is Phase 2.

title: Phase 2: Decompose monolith into parent + sub-components
content:
Split the v0 1749-line monolith into a parent custom element plus three sub-components, with no user-facing behaviour change. Implements Phase 2 of s-9gls.

## Tasks

- Create `src/diagram-viewer.js` registered as `diagram-viewer`: parent owns state, loads manifest, builds flat slide list, owns navigation history (back/forward stacks), wires children, exposes `loadData(data)` and `reset()` public API
- Create `src/diagram-canvas.js` registered as `diagram-canvas`: encapsulates iframe, load handler, SVG/image dimension detection, PNG-with-SVG-overlay handling, zoom level + zoom in/out/reset, mouse-wheel zoom, intercepts iframe link clicks and forwards via `slide-navigate` CustomEvent
- Create `src/diagram-nav-tree.js` registered as `diagram-nav-tree`: sidebar header, collapse/expand button, attribution footer, recursive nav tree of `layers/children/steps`, step bullets, active-item highlighting, click → `slide-select` CustomEvent
- Create `src/diagram-help-modal.js` registered as `diagram-help-modal`: native `<dialog>` with keyboard shortcut reference, `open()` / `close()` methods
- Move all CSS out of JS template-strings into `src/styles.css` so esbuild bundles a single `dist/diagram-viewer.css`; sub-component shadow-DOM styles stay inside their component files
- Parent reacts to child events: `slide-select` (sidebar click) → reset history, navigate; `slide-navigate` (iframe click) → push history, navigate; sub-components receive state via attributes/properties
- Preserve every v0 user-facing behaviour: keyboard nav (Up/Down/Left/Right/Space/Home/End/=/-/0/f/?/Esc), URL-hash sync, attribute-driven initial state (`manifest`, `base-path`, `sidebar`, `zoom`, `start-at`), sidebar resize handle, fullscreen toggle, error states for missing/invalid manifest

## Definition of done

`task build` produces `dist/diagram-viewer.{js,css}`. `task serve` renders the Kubernetes example and every v0 feature works (manual smoke check). No new features added; this phase is pure refactor.

title: Phase 3: localStorage persistence
content:
Add versioned localStorage persistence for both UI state and the manifest. Implements Phase 3 of s-9gls.

## Tasks

- Define a single versioned snapshot under storage key `diagramViewer.v1` with: `version: 1`, `manifest` (full v0-shape object or null when loaded by URL only), `basePath`, `ui: { currentSlideId, zoomPercent, sidebarOpen, sidebarWidthPx }`
- Add `_loadFromStorage()` and `_persist()` (debounced 250 ms, identical pattern to story-mapper) to `<diagram-viewer>`
- On `connectedCallback`: try snapshot first; if a snapshot exists, restore manifest + UI state and skip the manifest fetch; if no snapshot, fall back to the `manifest` attribute fetch
- Persist on every state change: slide change, zoom change, sidebar toggle, sidebar resize end (not during drag)
- On reload, restore current slide via the saved `currentSlideId` instead of the URL hash if both are present and differ — URL hash still wins on explicit hashchange events
- Add a `reset()` method that clears `localStorage.diagramViewer.v1`, drops the in-memory snapshot, and re-runs the original load path (re-fetch the `manifest` attribute or re-apply the last `loadData` payload)

## Definition of done

Manual reload restores the exact slide, zoom level, and sidebar state. `reset()` returns the viewer to its original source data. localStorage stores both UI state and the full manifest as designed in s-9gls.

title: Phase 4: JSON import/export dialog + Reset toolbar button
content:
Add a thin toolbar with `JSON` and `Reset` buttons that expose the snapshot via a native `<dialog>`. Implements Phase 4 of s-9gls.

## Tasks

- Add a top-right `JSON` toolbar button inside `<diagram-viewer>` that opens a native `<dialog>` containing a single `<textarea>`, plus `Copy`, `Apply`, and `Close` buttons and an inline error region (story-mapper pattern)
- On open, populate the textarea with `JSON.stringify(this._snapshot, null, 2)`
- `Copy` writes the textarea contents to the clipboard via `navigator.clipboard.writeText` with a `document.execCommand("copy")` fallback and a transient "Copied" indicator
- `Apply` parses the textarea; on parse error or `version !== 1`, show an inline error and do NOT mutate state; on valid parse, replace `this._snapshot` wholesale, persist, close the dialog, and re-render — full-replace, not merge
- Add a `Reset` toolbar button that calls the new `reset()` method (defined in Phase 3)
- Both toolbar buttons live in a thin toolbar above the existing sidebar/canvas grid so they stay visible without competing with the sidebar collapse button

## Definition of done

The dialog opens, exports a copy-pasteable snapshot, and applies a hand-crafted snapshot on demand. Invalid JSON or wrong version surfaces an inline error and leaves the existing snapshot untouched.

title: Phase 5: Public API loadData(data) (v0 shape only)
content:
Implement a public `loadData(data)` API on `<diagram-viewer>`. Implements Phase 5 of s-9gls.

## Tasks

- Implement `loadData(data)` on `<diagram-viewer>` that accepts the v0 manifest shape only — `{ name?, title?, version?, generated?, description?, layers: [{ id, title, path, type, children?, steps?, overlay? }] }`; reject anything else with a clear error
- If `loadData` is called and a snapshot already exists in localStorage, treat the new data as the source-of-truth manifest, refresh the manifest field of the snapshot, but preserve the user UI state (current slide if still present, zoom, sidebar) — orphaned `currentSlideId` falls back to `start-at` or `overview`
- Keep the source data in `_sourceData` so `reset()` can re-apply it without a refetch (matches story-mapper)

## Definition of done

`document.querySelector("diagram-viewer").loadData(manifestObject)` renders the supplied manifest. Calling `loadData` with a non-v0 shape (e.g. `{ images: [...] }`) raises a clear error and leaves state untouched. `reset()` after `loadData` re-applies the same payload without a refetch.

title: Phase 6: Playwright-go test suite (one file per component)
content:
Add a full Playwright-go end-to-end suite, split one file per component, sharing a single TestMain. Implements Phase 6 of s-9gls. Once this issue closes, post a summary implementation feedback to s-9gls covering Playwright results across all four files.

## Tasks

### Shared scaffold
- Create `tests/main_test.go` with shared setup: `TestMain` mirroring story-mapper (install playwright, launch headless Chromium, serve project root via `httptest.NewServer`, share browser across tests), plus shared helpers `newPage(t)`, `navigateToIndex(t, page)` (waits for `diagram-viewer` to attach), `clearLocalStorage(t, page)`, `loadFixture(t, page, path)`

### tests/diagram_viewer_test.go (parent)
- `loadData()` accepts v0 shape and renders
- `loadData()` rejects non-v0 shapes with a clear error
- JSON dialog export round-trips the snapshot
- JSON dialog import full-replaces the snapshot and re-renders
- JSON dialog import error keeps the existing snapshot intact
- Reset button clears localStorage and re-applies source data
- localStorage persistence across reload (current slide + zoom + sidebar state restored)

### tests/diagram_canvas_test.go (canvas)
- Zoom keys (`=` / `-` / `0`) update the zoom % readout
- Mouse-wheel + Ctrl zooms
- Iframe loads SVG and sets dimensions
- Iframe loads PNG-with-overlay (PNG as background, overlay SVG as iframe `src`)
- Iframe link click is intercepted and dispatches `slide-navigate`
- ArrowLeft (back) returns to the previous slide after an iframe-link click pushes history

### tests/diagram_nav_tree_test.go (sidebar)
- Clicking a nav item updates the iframe `src` and URL hash and moves the active class
- Expand/collapse toggles a sub-tree without navigating
- Sidebar collapse + toggle button round-trip
- Sidebar resize handle changes width and the new width persists
- Keyboard nav (ArrowDown / ArrowUp / Home / End) advances through the flat slide list and updates the active sidebar item

### tests/diagram_help_modal_test.go (help)
- Pressing `?` opens the `<dialog>`
- Pressing `Esc` closes it
- Clicking the close button closes it
- The modal lists every documented keyboard shortcut

## Definition of done

`task test` passes the full suite. Each test file targets the user-visible surface of its component, exercised through the real DOM (clicks, keys, dialogs) rather than private methods. Implementation feedback added to s-9gls summarising results.

title: Diagram Viewer v1 — post-rebuild bug fixes (epic)
content: Fix six post-rebuild bugs in <diagram-viewer>: parent-of-active-step highlighted, sticky :focus blue outline after click, PNG/SVG-overlay misalignment, JSON dialog centred to viewport instead of host, JSON button in wrong toolbar, and Reset button having no visible effect. Move both JSON and Reset to the sidebar footer; remove the top toolbar entirely. See TODO.md at project root and spec s-9gls.

title: Bug 1: Nav-tree — stop highlighting parent of active step
content: In src/diagram-nav-tree.js #updateActiveHighlight, drop the parentId branch from the .nav-item loop so only the active item gets .active. Keep the step-bullet parent-aware highlight as-is. Remove the parentId argument from setActive(slideId, parentId) (ignore extra args) and update the call site in src/diagram-viewer.js#loadSlide to pass only slide.id. Verify on the Kubernetes fixture: navigating to pod-lifecycle-step-1 shows active background only on the step bullet, not on the Pod Lifecycle parent. Recorded in TODO.md task 1; spec s-9gls.

title: Bug 2: Nav-tree — kill sticky :focus blue outline after click
content: In src/diagram-nav-tree.js styles, add .nav-item:focus { outline: none; } and .nav-item:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--color-primary, #6366f1); outline-offset: -2px; } so click never leaves a sticky outline but Tab keyboard focus still has a visible ring. Verify: click a heading (only active background, no outline); ↓/↑ keyboard nav (no stranded outline on the previously-clicked heading); Tab focus still shows a ring. Recorded in TODO.md task 2; spec s-9gls.

title: Bug 3: Canvas — align SVG overlay with PNG background
content: In src/diagram-canvas.js, inject zero-margin styles into the iframe document for both the PNG-with-overlay path (#handleImageInIframe → #handleSvgDimensions on the second load) and the plain SVG path (#handleSvgDimensions). Inject html, body { margin: 0; padding: 0; } svg { display: block; } via a <style data-viewer> element, guarded by the existing one-shot check used in #renderImage. Mirrors v0 expectation that the SVG content sits flush with the iframe top-left so the PNG background-image (contain, top-left) aligns. Verify on Kubernetes fixture cloud-controller-manager at zoom 50/150/300 % that the red overlay rectangles sit exactly on top of their PNG counterparts; etcd (plain SVG) still renders correctly. Recorded in TODO.md task 3; spec s-9gls.

title: Bug 5: Move JSON + Reset to sidebar footer; remove top toolbar
content:
In src/diagram-nav-tree.js, add JSON and Reset text buttons to the .sidebar-footer left-side group next to the attribution; styled to match .zoom-btn (font-size 0.6875rem, padding 0.125rem 0.5rem, height 1.5rem, border-radius 0.25rem). Click handlers dispatch json-open and reset CustomEvents (bubbles: true, composed: true). In src/diagram-viewer.js, remove the entire
…
block from #render, drop the .toolbar / .toolbar button CSS rules, and drop the toolbar row from grid-template-areas / grid-template-rows. In #initEventListeners, replace the toolbar-button click bindings with this.#navTree.addEventListener('json-open', …) and ('reset', …). Rename #initToolbar → #initJsonDialog (it now only wires the dialog's Copy/Apply/Close). Verify: top toolbar is gone; sidebar footer order is JSON · Reset · attribution · zoom controls · help; both buttons fire correctly. Recorded in TODO.md task 4; spec s-9gls.

title: Bug 4: JSON dialog — centre within host, not viewport
content: In src/diagram-viewer.js, replace native <dialog>/showModal() with a backdrop+modal pattern matching <diagram-help-modal>: <div class=json-dialog-backdrop> wrapping the existing .json-dialog content, position: absolute; inset: 0 over the .container (which already has position: relative). Centre with display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center on the backdrop. Modal panel keeps max-width 40rem, max-height 80vh, width 90 %. Toggle .open class instead of showModal()/close(). Backdrop click outside the panel closes it. Esc close: extend #handleKeyDown to close the JSON dialog before help-modal close path runs; track open state via the backdrop's class. Verify: JSON button opens the dialog perfectly centred over the <diagram-viewer> host even when the host is smaller than the viewport; backdrop click and Esc both close it. Depends on Bug 5 (the JSON button now lives in the sidebar). Recorded in TODO.md task 5; spec s-9gls.

title: Bug 6: Reset actually resets zoom + sidebar + URL hash
content: In src/diagram-viewer.js#reset, before the existing loadData(this.#sourceData) call, reset all UI state explicitly: restore #zoomLevel to default (parseInt(getAttribute('zoom'), 10) / 100 if valid, else 1.5); open the sidebar (remove sidebar-collapsed class); clear inline gridTemplateColumns; clear URL hash via history.replaceState(null, '', location.pathname + location.search); push reset zoom into the canvas (zoomLevel setter) and into the nav-tree (zoomPercent setter). After this the existing loadData call walks the no-snapshot, no-hash path and lands on start-at (default overview). Verify: navigate to cloud-controller-manager, zoom to 300 %, collapse sidebar; click Reset → land on overview, sidebar open at default width, zoom 150 %, URL hash cleared. Recorded in TODO.md task 6; spec s-9gls.

title: Bug-fix tests: nav-tree, viewer, canvas regression coverage
content: Update existing Playwright-go suites for the bug-fix work: tests/diagram_nav_tree_test.go — replace any selector that targeted the removed top .toolbar JSON/Reset buttons with sidebar-footer selectors; add a regression test that an active step's parent does NOT carry the active class. tests/diagram_viewer_test.go — switch from dialog.open checks to backdrop-class checks (.json-dialog-backdrop.open); add a regression test for Reset clearing zoom + sidebar + hash + slide. tests/diagram_canvas_test.go — add a regression test that loads cloud-controller-manager and asserts the iframe document body has zero margin (proxy for overlay alignment). Run task test until all four files pass. This is the leaf issue — provides summary implementation feedback to spec s-9gls on close. Recorded in TODO.md task 7; spec s-9gls.

feedback.content: Leaf issue for the post-rebuild bug-fix iteration. Summary implementation feedback expected on this spec when i-926o closes: nav-tree fixes (bug 1+2), canvas overlay alignment (bug 3), toolbar relocation (bug 5), JSON dialog re-centring (bug 4), Reset semantics (bug 6), and Playwright-go regression coverage. Companion to the original i-7yvf phase-6 feedback anchor.

Leaf issue for the post-rebuild bug-fix iteration. Summary implementation feedback expected on this spec when i-926o closes: nav-tree fixes (bug 1+2), canvas overlay alignment (bug 3), toolbar relocation (bug 5), JSON dialog re-centring (bug 4), Reset semantics (bug 6), and Playwright-go regression coverage.

title: Bug-fix regression epic — Bug 2 & 3 rework
content: Parent epic for two regression follow-ups under spec s-8sat. Original fixes under s-448f shipped (commits 485882a and 36c6e81) but did not fully resolve the user-visible behaviour. Bug 2 follow-up: blur on click in nav-tree so click never leaves DOM focus, preventing arrow-key promotion to :focus-visible. Bug 3 follow-up: null-guard iframeDoc.head in #handleSvgDimensions because direct-loaded SVG documents have no <head>/<body>. Plus a tests task that runs after both. See TODO.md.

title: Bug 2 follow-up: blur clicked nav link to prevent arrow-key focus-visible promotion
content: In src/diagram-nav-tree.js #createNavItem, in the existing link click handler, add link.blur() after dispatching slide-select. Reproduction (verified live with Playwright + real mouse + keyboard): click etcd in the sidebar → :focus matches but :focus-visible does NOT (good); press ArrowDown → parent's #handleKeyDown moves .active class to next slide, but the original etcd link STILL has DOM focus, and the arrow-key press promotes :focus to :focus-visible — outline rgb(99,102,241) solid 2px appears on etcd while its background reverts to hover gray. Blur on click breaks this chain. Keep the existing :focus { outline: none } and :focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--color-primary) } rules — they remain correct for Tab a11y. Verify: click etcd, press ↓ — no blue outline anywhere on etcd; only the new .active highlight (kube-controller-manager) is visible. Verify: Tab through nav items from a fresh page — tabbed items still show focus ring. TODO.md task 8; spec s-8sat.

title: Bug 3 follow-up: null-guard iframeDoc.head for direct-loaded SVG documents
content: In src/diagram-canvas.js #handleSvgDimensions, change the style-injection guard from 'if (iframeDoc && !iframeDoc.head.querySelector("style[data-viewer]"))' to 'if (iframeDoc?.head && !iframeDoc.head.querySelector("style[data-viewer]"))' so a missing head does not throw. Add a comment noting that browsers do not wrap a directly-loaded SVG in HTML — documentElement is the <svg> itself, with no <head>/<body>, and no body-margin issue exists in that case so skipping the injection is correct. Reproduction (verified live with Playwright on cloud-controller-manager): iframeDoc.documentElement.tagName === 'svg', iframeDoc.head === null, the property access throws TypeError, caught by outer try/catch in #handleIframeLoad, falls through to #setDefaultDimensions() which sets iframe to 100% × 100% (~1500×800) instead of the SVG's 877×704 — PNG (contain-fit to the huge iframe) renders much bigger than SVG content, hence "way off top left". Verify on Kubernetes fixture cloud-controller-manager at zoom 50/150/300 %: iframe size matches SVG dimensions (877×704), PNG fills the same area via contain, red overlay rectangles sit exactly on top of their PNG counterparts. Verify on plain SVG slide etcd: still renders correctly; no regression. TODO.md task 9; spec s-8sat.

title: Regression tests: keep body-margin check + add iframe-dimensions-match-SVG check
content: Update tests/diagram_canvas_test.go for both robustness and the real defect surface. Keep the existing body-margin-zero check from the original Bug 3 commit (covers the wrapped-in-HTML case some browsers may produce in the future or for non-SVG content with overlay) AND add a new assertion that on cloud-controller-manager the iframe element style.width equals the SVG's intrinsic width attribute (877) — that is a more direct proxy for alignment than body-margin and catches the real defect (iframe sized via setDefaultDimensions fallback at 100% × 100% instead of via setDimensionsAndScale). Add a parallel test for cloud-controller-manager that the iframe document body has zero margin OR is null (direct-loaded SVG). Also add a regression test for Bug 2 follow-up: click a nav-item, press ArrowDown, assert the previously-clicked link does not match :focus-visible (use page.evaluate to check). Run task test until all four files pass. This is the leaf — provides summary implementation feedback to spec s-8sat on close. TODO.md task 10; spec s-8sat.

feedback.content: Leaf issue for the bug-fix regression iteration. Summary implementation feedback expected on this spec when i-2o0x closes: Bug 2 (blur on click), Bug 3 (null-guard iframeDoc.head), and the two new regression tests (iframe-dimensions-match-SVG and post-arrow-key :focus-visible absence).

title: Multi-instance encapsulation epic
content:
Rollup issue tracking the seven-task implementation of spec s-7ubt (multi-instance encapsulation for <diagram-viewer>).

## Linear blocks chain

  i-20ih → i-139y → i-5zjh → i-6r8b → i-7is1 → i-6xqh → i-2ess → i-9tby (this)

Run 'sudocode ready' for the next unblocked task.

## Closure condition

This epic becomes ready (unblocked) once the leaf i-2ess (Playwright tests) closes — at which point the feedback anchor fires onto spec s-7ubt and the failing→passing criterion is provably met.

Close this epic only when:
1. all 7 task issues are closed,
2. 'task test' is green on the post-fix codebase,
3. the multi-instance demo at examples/multi.html visibly works in a real browser.

After this epic closes, manually close spec s-7ubt and dismiss/resolve the feedback record.

feedback.content: All 7 tasks completed and verified. Multi-instance encapsulation working: per-instance storage, keyboard scoping, bookmarkable hash, reset isolation, CSS encapsulation, double-define guards. 8/8 Playwright tests pass.

title: Per-instance identity (#instanceId)
content:
Resolve a stable per-instance identity in src/diagram-viewer.js connectedCallback BEFORE #loadFromStorage:

- if this.id (HTML id attribute) is non-empty → use verbatim
- else → FNV-1a 32-bit hash of (manifest attr + base-path attr), 8-char hex
- else → crypto.randomUUID() (non-persistent fallback)

Surface as data-instance-id on the host for debuggability. All subsequent state code must read this.#storageKey() which returns `diagramViewer.v1:${instanceId}`.

title: Namespaced localStorage and reset isolation
content:
Replace the module-level STORAGE_KEY = 'diagramViewer.v1' constant in src/diagram-viewer.js with a private #storageKey() method returning `diagramViewer.v1:${this.#instanceId}`.

Update all read/write sites: #loadFromStorage, #persist, loadData (preserve-UI fetch), reset (removeItem).

reset() removes ONLY this instance's key — never localStorage.clear(), never another viewer's key.

One-time legacy migration: on startup, if unnamespaced 'diagramViewer.v1' exists AND this is the only viewer on the page AND no namespaced key for this instance exists, migrate (read → write namespaced → delete legacy). Skip migration when more than one viewer is present so the stale legacy key never gets adopted by an arbitrary viewer.

title: Opt-in URL hash via bookmarkable attribute
content:
Add two new boolean attributes: bookmarkable (default OFF) and primary (default OFF, only meaningful when bookmarkable).

Add private #ownsHash() returning true only when this instance is bookmarkable AND (no other bookmarkable viewer exists, OR this is the one with primary). Resolve once on connect; re-resolve on attribute change.

Gate every hash interaction on #ownsHash():
- globalThis.addEventListener('hashchange', …) registration in #initEventListeners
- history.replaceState(null, '', newHash) write in #loadSlide
- history.replaceState in reset()
- location.hash.slice(1) reads in #loadFromHash, #loadFromStorage, loadData — fall back to storage or start-at when not owning the hash

console.warn when more than one bookmarkable viewer exists and none has primary.

When start-at is unset and no snapshot exists and viewer doesn't own the hash, default to the first slide in the flat list ('overview' may not exist in user manifests).

This is a documented breaking change for single-instance users who relied on URL hash → slide; they must add bookmarkable to keep that behaviour.

title: Scope keyboard to focus-within
content:
Replace the hover-based keyboard scoping with :focus-within in src/diagram-viewer.js.

- Remove mouseenter/mouseleave listeners on #container and the #enableKeyboardHandling / #disableKeyboardHandling pair that adds/removes a document keydown listener.
- Replace with a single per-instance keydown listener attached to the host element (this) in capture phase via the existing AbortController signal — focusable controls in the shadow root bubble into the host's keydown.
- Make the host focusable: tabindex='-1' set programmatically on connect; add :host(:focus-within) outline rule (or none) for visual parity.
- Re-route iframe-keydown CustomEvent from <diagram-canvas> through the same #handleKeyDown entry point.
- Verify: arrow keys with focus inside instance A only navigate A; same keys after Tab into instance B only navigate B; with focus elsewhere neither navigates.

title: Eliminate residual global escapes
content:
Close the remaining encapsulation leaks across the four component files.

- src/diagram-canvas.js: replace both globalThis.focus() calls in #setupIframeEventHandlers with this.focus({ preventScroll: true }) (the canvas element itself in this instance's shadow). Falls back to no-op if not focusable.
- src/diagram-viewer.js #initResizeHandle: change document.addEventListener('mousemove'/'mouseup', …) to attach only when a drag starts (in mousedown) and detach in mouseup / on AbortController abort — avoids N persistent document listeners for N instances.
- Audit all dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent(..., { bubbles: true, composed: true })) calls; document the cross-shadow-boundary intent in the file headers (no code change unless an event should not be composed).
- Guard every customElements.define(name, ctor) call (diagram-viewer.js, diagram-canvas.js, diagram-nav-tree.js, diagram-help-modal.js) with: if (!customElements.get(name)) customElements.define(name, ctor); — loading the bundle twice (ES module + script tag, microfrontend, hot-reload) must NOT throw NotSupportedError. If a different constructor is already registered under the same name, log a single console.warn naming the conflict and skip re-defining (do not throw).
- Confirm static #styles CSSStyleSheet shared via adoptedStyleSheets is read-only at runtime; add a code comment so future changes don't introduce per-instance mutation of the shared sheet.

title: Multi-instance demo (examples/multi.html)
content:
Create examples/multi.html mounting two viewers side-by-side in a CSS grid:
- left:  <diagram-viewer id='left'  base-path='examples/kubernetes' start-at='overview'> — no bookmarkable
- right: <diagram-viewer id='right' base-path='examples/kubernetes' start-at='kube-controller-manager' bookmarkable primary> — owns the hash

Bootstrap fetches examples/kubernetes/manifest.json once and calls loadData(data) on both.

Self-documenting captions above each viewer naming the instance and noting whether it owns the hash.

Add a 'See multi-instance demo' footer line linking from index.html, plus a one-line ## Multi-instance section in README.md.

title: Multi-instance Playwright tests (leaf)
content:
Add tests/diagram_viewer_multi_test.go with helpers analogous to main_test.go's navigateToIndex but pointing at /examples/multi.html, with per-instance accessors keyed by id.

Each test must FAIL on the pre-fix codebase and PASS after:

- storage isolation — zoom left to 300%, right to 50%, reload page; assert each retains own zoom (pre-fix: last-writer wins)
- slide isolation — navigate left to etcd, right to kube-scheduler; assert distinct active items in each nav-tree (pre-fix: hashchange cross-talk)
- hash ownership — only the primary bookmarkable instance updates location.hash on navigation; the other never does

title: Keyboard regression epic
content:
Rollup issue tracking the two-task implementation of spec s-79cq (keyboard regression after sidebar click).

## Linear blocks chain

title: Replace link.blur() with host-focus shift
content:
Restore the keyboard listener path after a sidebar click without reintroducing the :focus-visible outline that i-a68b removed.

## Changes

- src/diagram-nav-tree.js #createNavItem: remove the link.blur(); call after the slide-select dispatch.
- src/diagram-viewer.js #initEventListeners: add this.focus({ preventScroll: true }); at the end of the existing slide-select listener (after this.#navigateToId(...)).

## Why this works

The viewer host already has tabindex='-1' (set in connectedCallback for spec s-7ubt Task 4), so .focus() succeeds. The host has no :focus-visible outline rule, so focus on the host is visually invisible. The host IS in the keydown event path — subsequent ArrowDown / ArrowUp / +/-/0/?/Esc / Home / End fire #handleKeyDown.

The clicked nav link no longer holds focus, so :focus-visible cannot apply to it on the next keypress (the original i-a68b bug stays fixed).

## Manual verification

On the Kubernetes fixture:
- Click 'etcd' in the sidebar — no blue outline on etcd; the active background highlight is the only visual indicator.
- Press ArrowDown — slide advances to the next item.
- Press ArrowUp — returns to etcd.
- Verify iframe-keydown path: click inside the iframe content, press ArrowDown — still works (existing iframe-keydown re-dispatch unaffected).
- Multi-instance: examples/multi.html, click etcd in left viewer; ArrowDown advances ONLY left; right viewer's slide and zoom remain unchanged.

  - load index.html, clear localStorage, load Kubernetes fixture
  - click the 'etcd' nav link via shadow-DOM piercing JS
  - read the current active slide id; assert it is 'etcd'
  - call page.Keyboard().Press('ArrowDown')
  - read the new active slide id; assert it differs from 'etcd' (slide advanced)

- TestMulti_KeyboardWorksAfterSidebarClickInOneInstance (in tests/diagram_viewer_multi_test.go):
  - load examples/multi.html
  - click a nav link in the LEFT viewer (no explicit .focus() JS call — the click alone must arm the keyboard)
  - press ArrowDown
  - assert left viewer's active slide changed and right viewer's did NOT

## Existing guards (verify still pass)

- TestCanvas_NavClickThenArrowDownNoFocusVisible — confirms previously-clicked link does NOT match :focus-visible after ArrowDown (guards i-a68b).

## Procedure

Run 'task test' on the post-fix codebase — all four test files pass.

For provenance of the red→green gate, run the new tests against the pre-fix code first to verify the failure mode, then run against the post-fix code to verify pass. Record both results in the issue's closing comment.

This is the leaf — provides summary implementation feedback to s-79cq on close (feedback anchor attached at the 'Failing → Passing criterion' section).

title: Add #clearManifestError() helper and wire call sites in <diagram-viewer>
content:
Implements Task 1 of TODO.md, part of s-6em7. Foundation child — no incoming blockers.

In `src/diagram-viewer.js`:

1. Add private method `#clearManifestError()` that:
   - Removes every direct-child `.error` element from `this.#container` (use `:scope > .error` selector to scope removals to direct children only).
   - Restores `this.#canvas.style.display = ""` (only when currently `"none"`).
   - Removes the `sidebar-collapsed` class from `this.#container` so the recovered UI is fully usable per user direction (option 1 from clarifying questions).

2. Call `this.#clearManifestError()` at the START of `loadData(data)`, BEFORE any other mutation. This is the single recovery checkpoint — both `loadFromUrl()` (which calls `loadData()` on success) and direct external `loadData(data)` callers benefit.

3. Call `this.#clearManifestError()` at the TOP of `#showManifestError()` BEFORE appending the new error node, so consecutive failures do not stack overlay nodes (idempotency).

Verification by hand on `index.html`:
- bad path → error appears
- bad path again → only ONE error node in shadow DOM
- good path → error gone, canvas visible, sidebar open, kubernetes nav tree populated

No public API change. `loadFromUrl()`, `loadData()`, `reset()`, `openJsonDialog()` signatures unchanged.

title: Validate manifest paths are raw (reject %xx)
content:
Implements R1 of s-5uyp.

In src/diagram-viewer.js loadData() (around lines 218-236), extend the per-layer validation loop to reject any path or overlay string containing a %xx percent-encoded sequence (regex /%[0-9A-Fa-f]{2}/).

- Throw with a message that names the offending field and item id, stating "manifest paths must be raw/unencoded — use a literal space, not %20".
- Apply recursively to item.children[*].path, item.steps[*].path, and item.overlay (mirroring #buildFlatSlideList recursion).
- Add unit-style assertion in an existing test file or a small new test that loadData() throws on %20 in a path.

title: Resolve slide paths via URL constructor
content:
Implements R2, R3, R4 of s-5uyp.

In src/diagram-viewer.js #buildFlatSlideList() (lines 769-803), replace every `${this.#basePath}/${item.path}` and `${this.#basePath}/${item.overlay}` and `${this.#basePath}/${step.path}` with a single helper #resolveSlideUrl(relativePath) that returns `new URL(relativePath, this.#resolvedBase()).href`.

Implement #resolvedBase() returning a URL string with a guaranteed trailing slash, computed once per loadData() call:
- If this.#basePath is empty, use document.baseURI.
- Otherwise use `new URL(this.#basePath.endsWith("/") ? this.#basePath : this.#basePath + "/", document.baseURI).href`.

Cache the resolved base on the instance (e.g. this.#resolvedBaseUrl) and reset it whenever loadData() or loadFromUrl() runs or base-path attribute changes.

Result: slide.path values are now fully-qualified absolute URL strings.

title: Update diagram-canvas URL → slide matching
content:
Implements R5 of s-5uyp. Depends on i-1z01 (slide.path is now absolute URL).

In src/diagram-canvas.js #resolveUrlToSlide() (lines 158-170):
- Replace decodeURIComponent(url.pathname) + indexOf(basePath) substring logic.
- Each slide.path is now an absolute URL string (from i-1z01), so resolve href via `new URL(href, baseUrl).href` and find the slide whose path equals that resolved URL.
- Drop this.#basePath from the matching code path entirely.
- Keep the try/catch wrapper and return null fallback.

Remove the now-unused basePath setter on diagram-canvas if no other code reads it; otherwise leave as a no-op with a code comment explaining why.

title: Audit other path concatenations in src/
content:
Implements R2 (completeness) of s-5uyp.

Search src/ for any remaining `${...}/${...}` template-literal patterns that build URLs (overlay handling in diagram-canvas.js, any nav-tree icon paths, etc.) and convert to `new URL()` resolution where the result is used as a network URL.

- Document any deliberate exceptions inline with a code comment.
- Confirm diagram-loader.js loadFromUrl() already uses `new URL(url, document.baseURI).href` (it does — keep as is).

title: Add Playwright test for spaces in paths
content:
Implements R6, R7 of s-5uyp.

Create tests/fixtures/spaced/ containing:
- manifest.json referencing a slide at "Control Plane/diagram.svg"
- directory "Control Plane/" with a tiny valid diagram.svg
- index.html mounting <diagram-viewer manifest="manifest.json" sidebar bookmarkable>

Add tests/diagram_spaced_paths_test.go that:
- serves the fixture via the existing test server harness (see main_test.go for pattern)
- loads the page in Playwright
- clicks the sidebar entry for the spaced-path slide
- captures the network request for the SVG
- asserts the request URL contains exactly one occurrence of %20 and zero occurrences of %2520

Add a second assertion: after navigation, location.hash matches the slide id (proves bookmarkable round-trip still works with the new absolute-URL slide.path).

Run `task test` and confirm all existing tests still pass alongside the new one.

title: Document manifest path rules in README
content:
Implements R8 of s-5uyp.

In README.md, add a short "Manifest path rules" subsection stating:
- Paths must be raw/unencoded relative paths (e.g. "Control Plane/x.svg", never "Control%20Plane/x.svg").
- The component resolves them against base-path (or the manifest URLs directory) using the browsers URL parser.
- Rationale: avoids double-encoding when served by static site generators that already percent-encode URLs in the surrounding HTML.

Also note the breaking change: slide.path in slide-change event details is now an absolute URL string, not a relative path.

title: Always call #loadManifest() in connectedCallback when manifest attribute is set
content: In src/diagram-viewer.js connectedCallback() (around lines 124–127), change load order: if this.hasAttribute('manifest'), call this.#loadManifest() unconditionally; only fall back to this.#loadFromStorage() (and then #loadManifest()) when no manifest attribute is present. Do not catch fetch errors and silently restore from storage — #loadManifest() continues to call #showManifestError() on failure. Report a one-paragraph summary of what was changed back to spec s-1y9j on completion via 'sudocode feedback add'.

feedback.content: Issue i-1ck4 completed: Changed connectedCallback() in src/diagram-viewer.js to unconditionally call this.#loadManifest() when the manifest attribute is present, bypassing localStorage restoration. When no manifest attribute is set, the original behavior is preserved: try localStorage first, then fall back to #loadManifest(). This ensures the manifest is always fetched fresh when explicitly configured, and any fetch errors continue to surface via #showManifestError() rather than being silently swallowed by a localStorage fallback.

title: Restrict localStorage merge in loadData() to UI state only
content: In src/diagram-viewer.js loadData() (around lines 296–308), confirm the existing snapshot read only consumes snapshot.ui and ignores snapshot.manifest and snapshot.basePath. Add a code comment: 'only UI state is merged from storage when a manifest is fetched; manifest and basePath are ignored to avoid stale state'. Confirm currentSlideId is discarded when not found in freshly built #flatSlides (around lines 337–344) and add a brief comment 'discard local currentSlideId on conflict'. Leave #loadFromStorage() unchanged for the no-manifest-attribute case. Report a one-paragraph summary back to spec s-1y9j via 'sudocode feedback add'.

title: Playwright test: stale-manifest bug refresh
content: Add fixture tests/fixtures/manifest_refresh/ with manifest.v1.json (slide id 'a'), manifest.v2.json (slide id 'b' only — no 'a'), and index.html mounting <diagram-viewer manifest='manifest.json' sidebar bookmarkable>. Add tests/diagram_manifest_refresh_test.go that: serves fixture with v1 as manifest.json, loads page, navigates to 'a', sets zoom to 200%, waits for localStorage write, swaps manifest.json to v2 content, reloads, asserts (1) nav tree contains 'b' and not 'a', (2) zoom level still 200% (UI state preserved), (3) displayed slide is 'b' (discarded conflicting currentSlideId). Run 'task test' and confirm the test passes. Report a one-paragraph summary back to spec s-1y9j via 'sudocode feedback add'.

feedback.content: Implemented i-11qg: Added tests/fixtures/manifest_refresh/ with manifest.v1.json (slide "a"), manifest.v2.json (slide "b"), index.html, and SVGs. Created tests/diagram_manifest_refresh_test.go that swaps manifest on disk mid-test and reloads, asserting (1) nav tree shows "b" not "a", (2) zoom preserved at 200%, (3) displayed slide is b.svg. All pass in full suite. The existing loadData code already handles this correctly—UI state preserved from localStorage, stale currentSlideId discarded when slide missing from new manifest.

title: Playwright test: fetch-failure surfaces error, no cached fallback
content: Add tests/diagram_manifest_fetch_error_test.go that: serves a fixture that successfully loads manifest.json once (writes localStorage), reloads the page with the server returning 500 (or unreachable URL) for manifest.json, asserts the manifest-error UI is visible and the cached manifest from storage is NOT rendered (nav tree empty / error shown). Run 'task test' to confirm pass. Report a one-paragraph summary back to spec s-1y9j via 'sudocode feedback add'.

feedback.content: Issue i-6pac implemented: Added tests/diagram_manifest_fetch_error_test.go with TestManifestFetchError_ShowsErrorNotCachedFallback. The test creates a custom httptest server serving manifest.json successfully on first request then returning HTTP 500 on subsequent requests. A fixture HTML with manifest attribute ensures connectedCallback fetches fresh (bypassing localStorage restore). Verifies: (1) localStorage populated after success, (2) .error UI visible after 500 response, (3) nav tree has zero items confirming no cached fallback. All 50 tests pass.

title: Document persistence semantics in README
content: In README.md, add a short 'Persistence' subsection stating: when the manifest attribute is set, manifest.json is fetched on every load; only UI state (zoom, sidebar open/width, current slide) is restored from localStorage; conflicting state (e.g. a current slide that no longer exists in the new manifest) is discarded; manifest content and base-path are never restored from storage. Report a one-paragraph summary back to spec s-1y9j via 'sudocode feedback add'.

title: Build cmd/manifest tool (epic)
content:
Parent epic for building the `cmd/manifest` utility. Implements [[s-78xv]]{ implements }.

Children execute in order via `blocks` edges. Provide summary implementation feedback on s-78xv when the leaf child closes and this epic transitions to ready.

See `TODO.md` in the project root for the full task list.

title: Scaffold cmd/manifest CLI and TDD harness
content:
Scaffold the command and its TDD harness. Part of s-78xv.

Tasks:
- Create `cmd/manifest/main.go` with `flag`-based CLI: `--in <path>` (required), `--out <dir>` (optional), `--format <d2>` (default `d2`).
- Support trailing `--` separator: args after `--` captured as `passthrough []string` and forwarded verbatim to the renderer (e.g. `go tool manifest --in x.d2 --out out -- --theme 200 --layout elk --sketch`).
- Add `cmd/manifest/manifest_test.go` with one failing test running against `examples/kubernetes.d2/` and asserting output deep-equals a golden file (red phase).
- Wire `manifest` into `go.mod` `tool (...)` block alongside caddy/esbuild so it runs as `go tool manifest`.
- Add Taskfile target `manifest:example` running the tool against `examples/kubernetes.d2/`.

Verification: `go tool manifest --help` prints usage; the failing test exists and fails for the expected reason (not implemented yet).

title: Define manifest types and JSON shape
content:
Define Go structs round-tripping the manifest schema. Part of s-78xv.

Tasks:
- In `cmd/manifest/types.go`, define `Node{ID, Title, Path, Type, Overlay?, Children?, Steps?}` and `Step{Step, Path, Title}`. Top-level metadata fields (`name`, `title`, `version`, `generated`, `description`) deliberately omitted in v1.
- Unit test: unmarshal existing `examples/kubernetes/manifest.json` into these types and re-marshal to confirm faithful shape (ignoring metadata fields).

Verification: round-trip test passes against the committed example.

title: Format dispatcher interface
content:
Format dispatcher with extensibility seam for future tools. Part of s-78xv.

Tasks:
- Add `cmd/manifest/format.go` with interface `Format { Build(inPath string) (*Node, error); Render(inPath, outDir string, passthrough []string) error }`. The `passthrough` slice carries args after `--`.
- Register `d2` as the only implementation in v1 (impl in next issue).
- Unknown `--format` values exit non-zero with a clear error.

Verification: test asserts `--format=mermaid` exits with `unsupported format "mermaid"` and exit code 2.

title: d2 graph walk → manifest tree
content:
Implement `Format.Build` for d2 by compiling the source and walking the resolved board hierarchy. Part of s-78xv.

Tasks:
- Add `cmd/manifest/d2.go` importing `oss.terrastruct.com/d2/d2lib` and `oss.terrastruct.com/d2/d2graph`.
- Implement `(d2Format).Build` using `d2lib.Compile` against the root `.d2` file. The compiler resolves `@imports` and `...@spread` natively — no manual import handling.
- Walk `*d2graph.Graph.Layers / Scenarios / Steps` recursively to produce the `Node` tree.
- Top-level node id hard-coded `"overview"`, path `"index.svg"`, matching the existing example.
- Derive child paths by joining ancestor sanitized board names with `/index.svg` (e.g. board `Control plane` → `Control Plane/index.svg`). Reproduce the d2 CLI sanitizer; verify against actual d2 output during render task.
- Map kinds: `Layers` → `layer` (leaf) or `scenario` (branch with nested layers); `Scenarios` → `scenario`; `Steps` → `steps` with populated `steps[]` (`{step: N, path: "<n>.svg", title: "Step N"}`).

Verification: walking the kubernetes example produces a tree shape matching the existing `examples/kubernetes/manifest.json` (paths and types).

title: id slugification
content:
Slugifier for the manifest `id` field. Part of s-78xv.

Tasks:
- Add `cmd/manifest/slug.go` with `slugify(name string) string`: lowercase, ASCII-fold, replace runs of non-alphanumerics with a single `-`, trim leading/trailing `-`.
- Use slugify only for `id`. `title` is the original board name verbatim.
- Unit tests covering: spaces, mixed case, existing hyphens, leading/trailing punctuation, repeated separators.
- Examples: `"Control plane"` → `"control-plane"`, `"kube-controller-manager"` → `"kube-controller-manager"`, `"Pod Lifecycle"` → `"pod-lifecycle"`.

Verification: slug tests pass; the d2 walk in i-7c5x uses `slugify` for ids only.

title: Overlay detection via *.overlay.d2 siblings
content:
Detect and attach overlay paths to manifest nodes. Part of s-78xv.

Tasks:
- After walking the graph, for every node whose source `.d2` board has a sibling `*.overlay.d2` file (e.g. `Control plane/cloud-controller-manager.d2` → `Control plane/cloud-controller-manager.overlay.d2`), set `Overlay` to the rendered overlay path (`Control Plane/cloud-controller-manager.overlay.svg`).
- Detection runs against the source filesystem (the dir containing the input `.d2` files), not the output dir, so it works in `--out`-omitted mode.
- During render (next issue), each detected `*.overlay.d2` is rendered to its sibling `*.overlay.svg` with the same passthrough flags so themes/layout match.
- Unit test using fixture `cmd/manifest/testdata/overlay/` with one board + one overlay.

Verification: fixture test asserts the overlay node has the correct `overlay` path; without `--out` no rendering happens but `overlay` is still populated.

title: Render via d2 binary with -- passthrough
content:
Shell out to the `d2` binary for SVG rendering, forwarding `--` passthrough flags. Part of s-78xv.

Tasks:
- In `(d2Format).Render`, locate the `d2` binary via `exec.LookPath("d2")`. If missing, fail with a clear message pointing at https://d2lang.com/tour/install.
- Invoke `d2 <passthrough...> <inPath> <outDir>` (passthrough flags placed before positional args so they apply globally — verify against `d2 --help`; adjust if any must come after). Stream stdout/stderr through.
- After success, render each detected overlay with `d2 <passthrough...> <overlay.d2> <overlay.svg>`.
- Test (skipped when `d2` not on PATH): run with `-- --theme 200` and assert the binary is invoked with that arg in the expected position. Use a fake `d2` script on PATH inside the test if needed to avoid a real render dependency.
- End-to-end check: run against the kubernetes example and diff `examples/kubernetes/` (filenames + directory structure) against the committed copy. Manifest paths must resolve to real files on disk.

Verification: forwarded-args test passes; end-to-end diff is clean (or path-derivation in i-7c5x updated to match).

title: Stdout vs file output modes
content:
Wire `--out` presence/absence into the two output modes. Part of s-78xv.

Tasks:
- If `--out` is empty: marshal the manifest to indented JSON and write to stdout; do NOT call `Render`. Passthrough args after `--` are still parsed without error but unused — warn on stderr if non-empty so users notice.
- If `--out` is set: ensure the directory exists, call `Render` (with passthrough), then write `manifest.json` into it.
- Test both modes: stdout mode produces JSON parseable as our `Node` type; file mode writes `<out>/manifest.json` and (in an integration test that skips when `d2` is not on PATH) at least one SVG file.

Verification: both mode tests pass; stderr warning appears in stdout-mode-with-passthrough scenario.

title: Golden-file integration test
content:
Pin the end-to-end output of the tool. Part of s-78xv.

Tasks:
- Commit `cmd/manifest/testdata/kubernetes.golden.json` derived from a manual run of the tool against `examples/kubernetes.d2/` (the unzipped example).
- The integration test from i-3a8z (scaffold) now compares actual output to this golden file; failure prints a unified diff.
- Add a `task manifest:regen-golden` Taskfile target so the golden can be regenerated intentionally.

Verification: integration test goes green; intentionally mutating the golden produces a readable diff failure.

title: Documentation for cmd/manifest
content:
Document the new tool. Final task in s-78xv. This is the leaf child — closing it transitions the parent epic i-9q8d to ready.

Tasks:
- Add a "Generating manifests" section to `README.md` documenting: install d2 binary, run `go tool manifest --in path/to/diagram.d2 --out path/to/out [-- <d2 flags>]`, the `*.overlay.d2` convention, and worked examples (e.g. `-- --theme 200 --layout elk`, `-- --sketch`).
- Note v1 limitations: only `d2` format; no top-level metadata (users add `name`/`title`/etc. by hand or via a wrapper).
- Cross-reference from the existing "Build" section.

Verification: README renders cleanly; each documented invocation has been run by hand and works.

When closing: provide summary implementation feedback on s-78xv covering what shipped, deviations from the spec, surprises (especially around d2 path-sanitization), and any follow-ups.

title: Cover-fit SVG rendering with viewBox-aware measurement (epic)
content:
Parent epic for the cover-fit + viewBox-aware-measurement fix.

Implements [[s-9ymk]]{ implements }.

## Children

1. Add failing Playwright fit tests (red phase)
2. Fix SVG dimension detection per spec order
3. Switch fit-scale to cover (Math.max)
4. Default zoom to 1.0 + update tests/docs
5. Recompute fit on viewer resize via ResizeObserver

## Closing checks

- [ ] All children closed
- [ ] `task test` passes
- [ ] Smoke test of `examples/kubernetes/`: cover-fit on first load, zoom controls work relative to new fit-scale, resize re-fits, tall diagrams (e.g. `Nodes/index.svg` 1202×2468) render at correct content size.

title: Add failing Playwright tests for viewBox-aware fit-to-shortest-side
content:
TDD red phase for the cover-fit feature. Implements R5 of s-9ymk.

## Tasks

1. Add `tests/fixtures/fit/viewbox_only.svg` containing only `<svg viewBox="0 0 1200 400" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">…</svg>` (no width/height attrs). Include some visible content (e.g. a rect) so the bounding box is non-empty.
2. Add `tests/fixtures/fit/percent_size.svg` with `width="100%" height="100%" viewBox="0 0 600 800"` to verify percentage attrs are skipped in favour of viewBox.
3. Add a minimal `tests/fixtures/fit/manifest.json` referencing both.
4. Add `tests/diagram_canvas_fit_test.go` that:
   - Loads each fixture in `<diagram-viewer>`.
   - Reads the iframe `dataset.baseWidth` / `dataset.baseHeight` via Playwright `evaluate` and asserts they equal the viewBox dimensions (1200/400 and 600/800), NOT the iframe viewport.
   - Asserts the computed inline `transform: scale(N)` matches `Math.max((viewerW - 32) / vbW, (viewerH - 32) / vbH)` (cover fit at zoom 1.0).
5. Both assertions MUST fail when run against current `main`. Capture the failing output in the PR description as evidence the test is meaningful.

## Acceptance

- New test file present, fixtures present, test compiles.
- Running `task test` (or equivalent) shows the new test failing on `main` with a clear assertion message.

## Notes

- Use 32px (= 2rem at default 16px font-size) for padding to match `#setDimensionsAndScale`.
- The viewport reading must come from the actual canvas client size at test time; do not hard-code.
- This issue ships a failing test on purpose — do NOT close it until the implementation issues land. Close after issue 5 (resize) is closed and all tests are green.

Refer to s-9ymk.

title: Fix SVG dimension detection (viewBox-aware, spec-order)
Switch fit-scale to cover (Math.max along shortest side)
Default zoom level 1.0 (was 1.5) and update tests/docs
content:
Implement R3 of s-9ymk: with the corrected fit-scale, the default zoom should be 1.0 (true fit-to-viewport), not 1.5.

## Implementation

In `src/diagram-canvas.js`:
1. Line 47 — change class field `#zoomLevel = 1.5` to `#zoomLevel = 1.0`.
2. Line 351 — change `this.#zoomLevel = 1.5` (first-load fallback when no `zoom` attr is set) to `this.#zoomLevel = 1.0`.

In `tests/diagram_viewer_test.go`:
- Lines 232-233 — update the assertion `if m["zoomLevel"].(string) != "150%"` and message `expected zoom reset to 150%%, got %s` to expect `"100%"`.

In `README.md` and any inline JSDoc:
- Search the repo for `1.5`, `150%`, "default zoom" mentions and update narrative to "1.0 (100%)".

## Acceptance

- New test `task test` run: all tests pass (including the updated 100% assertion and the cover-fit assertions from i-8w67 + i-6ls0).
- Manual: loading a slide for the first time shows the diagram at exact cover-fit (no extra 50% blow-up).
- README and JSDoc no longer reference 1.5/150% as the default.

## Notes

- `localStorage`-persisted user zoom values are unchanged (they were never tied to the default). Users with stored 1.5 will see overflow on those slides — acceptable, they can press `0`.

Refer to s-9ymk R3. Blocked by i-6ls0.
